  • Go to National Climate Assessment (NCA)

    Development of the Fifth National Climate Assessment (NCA5) is currently underway, with anticipated delivery in 2023. This content will be updated as new information becomes available.

    Mandate

    The U.S. Global Change Research Program (USGCRP) was established by Presidential initiative in 1989 and mandated by Congress in the Global Change Research Act (GCRA) of 1990. Its mandate is to develop and coordinate a comprehensive and integrated United States research program to understand, assess, predict, and respond to global change. USGCRP comprises 13 federal agencies that conduct or use research on global change and its impacts on society. It operates under the Subcommittee on Global Change Research of the National Science and Technology Council. Its responsibilities include coordinating research, producing mandated reports, informing decisions, and supporting international collaboration. The GCRA also mandates a quadrennial National Climate Assessment (NCA), which: - Integrates and interprets scientific findings - Analyzes impacts across sectors like agriculture, energy, transportation, and health - Projects trends over the next 25–100 years

    NCA5 Chapters

    Subject to change

    Regions
